mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, directly supporting higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer income, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows despite unemployment spikes, or forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal spending combined with stable or declining inflation indicates robust real demand growth without monetary erosion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >65% of months when INDPRO rises, or the signal reverses direction during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 2 million workers indicates severe economic contraction and reduced corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of months following large payroll declines, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is positive, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with rising consumer prices signal real negative interest rates that boost equity valuations through financial repression and asset substitution, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of triggered months, or CPIAUCSL increases fail to correlate with equity gains when rates are near zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts exceeding 100,000 units signals renewed confidence in long-term economic stability and construction sector demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >55% of months following large housing start increases, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods.
